guide_response.verdict: APPROVED_WITH_AMENDMENTS_BOUND -- Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint dispositions Item 3.4 STOP and binds remaining-Stage-3 spec corrections (Items 3.4 through 3.10.v2) under one new acknowledgment g7-ea1_n13 with eight sub-clauses (a-h). EA-1 may resume Stage 3 execution at Item 3.4 after restating g7-ea1_n13 verbatim with all sub-clauses.

summary.trigger_context: EA-1 reached Stage 3 Item 3.4 ("Populate devplatform pyproject.toml with platform dependencies") and STOPPED with a third compound spec drift in succession (after 3.1.v2 escalation_watchdog filename and 3.2.v2 governance reclassification). Spec assumed BlarAI/pyproject.toml has a [project] block with [project.dependencies] to filter; actual file is 30 lines, pytest-config-only, with no [project] table. The reactive STOP-cycle pattern (one verdict per drift) is not converging; each iteration surfaces a new class of finding.
summary.approach: Lead Architect approved a procedural restructuring: insert a Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint BEFORE EA-1 resumes Item 3.4. A fresh audit subagent (NOT EA-1; EA-1 is procedurally fatigued from three STOPs) was spawned by Guide-#7 to inventory all remaining Stage 3 items (3.4 through 3.10 + 3.10.v2) against actual disk state. Output: drift inventory by item, classified by drift type and severity, with cross-item cascade analysis. Audit ran ~48 minutes; consumed 170,332 tokens; performed 114 tool uses. Evidence artifact committed alongside this verdict at: `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_DRIFT_INVENTORY.md`
summary.findings_overview:
The audit identified drifts in eight remaining Stage 3 items, plus one new item (3.4.5) needed to close a previously-noted finding (F-N11 from the 3.2.v2 verdict, now promoted from Class D to Class C). Classifications: - Class A (data-loss / irreversible): 1 finding (Item 3.6 VIKUNJA_PASS plaintext + dual-schema preservation) - Class B (duplication-avoidance / functional): 4 findings (Items 3.4, 3.5, 3.7, 3.9) - Class C (functional regression): 3 findings (Items 3.4.5 NEW, 3.6 .vscode tasks.json broadening, 3.9 explicit allow-list staging) - Class D (cosmetic / audit-trail): 3 findings (Items 3.8, 3.10.v2, 3.8 inverse-verify)
All findings consolidated into one new acknowledgment g7-ea1_n13 with eight sub-clauses (a-h) bound via cumulative-ack inheritance (same vehicle as Class A binding for 3.2.v2).
summary.new_acknowledgment_summary: g7-ea1_n13 sub-clauses bind the following amendments (full text in §new_acknowledgment below): - n13(a) Item 3.4: empirical dep derivation `[httpx, pyyaml, mcp, jsonschema]` + test extras `[pytest, pytest-asyncio]`. NO `requests` (not used by any platform tool). - n13(b) Item 3.5: verify command `import yaml, httpx, mcp, jsonschema; print('OK')` (substitutes `import yaml, requests`). - n13(c) Item 3.6: dual-schema path rewrites (`mcpServers` for `.mcp.json`, `servers` for `.vscode/mcp.json`); VIKUNJA_PASS plaintext to `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` in both files; broaden scope to include `.vscode/tasks.json` (closes F-N9). - n13(d) Item 3.7: substitute destructive smoke test with `python -m tools.fleet_observability.daily_digest --help` (eliminates Vikunja task creation + BlarAI/docs/FLEET_DASHBOARD.md side effect; honors Stage 3 invariant). - n13(e) Item 3.9: gitignore augmentation (`tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/`, `tools/fleet_observability/escalation_seen.json`, `*.tmp`); explicit allow-list `git add` (replaces bare `git add .`). - n13(f) Item 3.4.5 NEW: copy `BlarAI/tools/tests/` to `devplatform/tools/tests/` (7 files: `__init__.py` + 6 test modules). F-N11 promotion from Class D to Class C. - n13(g) Item 3.10.v2: copy_manifest_v2.yaml schema definition (schema_version=1; v1_baseline_files + v2_added_files lists; totals block). - n13(h) Item 3.8: enrich verify with `-Recurse` and add inverse-verify asserting 3.2.v2 + 3.3 expanded copies are present.
summary.forward_carry: Five residual-risk items forwarded to Stage 4 EA primary audit (cannot be caught by this Stage 3 audit). Three forward-bound to Stage 5 EA (already-known F-N5/6/7/8/10 reinforced). One new Stage 6.7.5 hardening ticket added (TICKET E: this audit's amendments folded into posterity XML cleanup batch).
summary.residual_risk_acknowledgment: Drift coverage of this audit is approximately 85-90% per audit subagent §7 confidence estimate. The escalating-discovery pattern (each iter finds a new class) suggests 1-2 NEW drift classes may surface during EA-1 resumption execution. Defense-in-depth posture: bind Class A items aggressively NOW; accept Class B/C STOP-cycles as the cost of doing business if they surface. The Stage 4 EA's primary audit is the architectural backstop against any drift this checkpoint missed.

independent_disk_verification.audit_provenance: Audit performed by general-purpose subagent spawned by Guide-#7 at 2026-04-27. Read-only mandate; zero file mutations; no commits. Subagent observed disk state at devplatform HEAD `3894221` (matches expected) and BlarAI HEAD `97effe2` (advanced beyond prompt's stated `77e26a9`; advance was uncommitted-then-committed work between session start and audit invocation; non-blocking). Full subagent output preserved in evidence artifact. Cited findings below are verbatim or paraphrased from §3 PER_ITEM_FINDINGS.

item.disk_state: BlarAI/pyproject.toml (30 lines): contains ONLY `[tool.pytest.ini_options]`. No `[project]` table. No `[project.dependencies]`. No `[build-system]`. BlarAI/pyproject.toml testpaths: 14 entries spanning shared/, services/, launcher/, tests/integration/, AND tools/* — the testpaths list mixes BlarAI-runtime (services/*) with platform-tool (tools/*) directories. BlarAI/.venv site-packages: httpx 0.28.1, jsonschema 4.26.0, mcp 1.26.0, pytest 9.0.2, pytest-asyncio 1.3.0, PyYAML 6.0.3, requests 2.32.5 (plus httpx-sse, types-requests as transitive). Empirical import survey of platform tools (grep `^(import|from) (yaml|requests|httpx|pytest|jsonschema|psutil|toml|pydantic|aiohttp|mcp)\b`): - httpx: used by `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py:16`, `tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py:68`, `tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests/test_bridge.py:15` - mcp.server.fastmcp: used by `tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py:69` - pytest: used in 24 test files across `tools/` - yaml: lazy-imported inside try-blocks at `tools/_project_context.py:21`, `tools/autonomy_budget/active_tasks.py:96`, `tools/autonomy_budget/config_loader.py:105,179` - requests: NOT imported by any platform tool source file; only appears in `tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/*.log` (Claude session artifacts, not runtime).

devplatform/.venv/Lib/site-packages: only pip + setuptools + _distutils_hack + pkg_resources. yaml/requests/pytest/httpx/mcp/jsonschema all NOT installed. Verify command `import yaml, requests`: yaml would resolve if Item 3.4 binds pyyaml; requests would FAIL with ModuleNotFoundError because no platform tool needs requests.
item.severity: Class C (functional regression — verify will fail)

item.disk_state: BlarAI/.mcp.json (19 lines): top-level key `mcpServers` (capitalized, plural). VIKUNJA_PASS plaintext. Hardcoded paths to BlarAI/.venv/Scripts/python.exe + BlarAI as PYTHONPATH. BlarAI/.vscode/mcp.json (20 lines): top-level key `servers` (lowercase, no "mcp" prefix; DIFFERENT SCHEMA from .mcp.json — STATUS Anomaly A1). Each entry has `type: "stdio"`. Uses `${workspaceFolder}/.venv/Scripts/python.exe` (already portable). VIKUNJA_PASS plaintext. devplatform/.vscode/: EMPTY directory. devplatform/.mcp.json: does NOT exist. Stage 3 spec at item 3.6 does not disambiguate which schema applies to which file. EA-1 must preserve each file's existing top-level key. `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` syntax compatibility verified across all three consumers: Claude Desktop (claude_desktop_config.json), Claude Code (.mcp.json), VS Code Copilot (.vscode/mcp.json) — all support env-var substitution natively.
item.severity: Class A (secret leak prevention) + Class C (schema cross-pollination would silently break VS Code MCP loader)

item.disk_state: Preflight regex `'blarai_project_id:\s*(\d+)'` matches BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml line 1 (`blarai_project_id: 3`); preflight will succeed. Module `tools.fleet_observability.daily_digest` exists at devplatform/tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py (byte-identical to BlarAI source). argparse parser at daily_digest.py lines 147-163 defines ONLY `--project-root` and `--project-id`. `--dry-run` flag does NOT exist anywhere in any platform tool. Grep `dry.run` across `tools/fleet_observability/*.py` returns zero hits. daily_digest.main() at lines 166-196: unconditionally calls `client.create_task(...)` to write into Vikunja project 4 (BlarAI Infrastructure). Also calls `dashboard_md.refresh(digest_body=body)` at line 193 which writes to docs/FLEET_DASHBOARD.md at ctx.root (BlarAI per --project-root arg). Vikunja server is running (per g6-ea6_n8 Option B; V4=1 host process). Spec's `--dry-run` clause was the safety mechanism; clause references a flag that doesn't exist.
item.severity: Class B/C (creates Vikunja task + modifies BlarAI working tree; violates Stage 3 invariant "BlarAI source untouched")

item.disk_state: find devplatform/docs/ -maxdepth 3 -type f filtered by `Task4|P5_|ADR|LEDGER|IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN|TEST_GOVERNANCE` (case-insensitive): 0 hits. Verify will PASS. Stage 3.2.v2 + 3.3 expanded copies verified present: - devplatform/docs/governance/ (4 files: fleet-hygiene, merge-policy, parallel-sprints, STYLE) - devplatform/docs/runbooks/ (13 files: 12 BlarAI runbooks + F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK) - devplatform/docs/scheduled/wake_templates/ (6 files) - devplatform/docs/domain8_spike_findings.md - devplatform/docs/claude_cowork/ (1 file: 01_EA_COWORK_INSTRUCTIONS.md) - devplatform/docs/claude_projects/ (6 files) - top-level CLAUDE_*/CO_LEAD_*/DEC*/DOMAIN*/CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md present Verify command's Get-ChildItem | Select-String pipeline iterates only immediate children (no -Recurse); shallow but mitigated by absence of nested BlarAI-only files.
item.severity: Class D (cosmetic; no functional risk given empirical disk state)

item.disk_state: cd devplatform && git status: clean working tree at 3894221. Untracked: docs/, tools/ (work products of items 3.1-3.6). Once 3.4-3.8 produce additional changes, those will also be tracked. devplatform/.gitignore (16 lines): covers __pycache__/, *.pyc, .venv/, .pytest_cache/, .mypy_cache/, *.log, tools/vikunja/*.db, tools/vikunja/files/, tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/state.json, tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/processed.json, .env, .env.local, tools/autonomy_budget/state.json.lock, tools/autonomy_budget/state.json.tmp. Gitignore gaps surfaced relative to BlarAI/.gitignore: - tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/ NOT in devplatform/.gitignore (BlarAI line 137 covers it) - tools/fleet_observability/escalation_seen.json NOT in devplatform/.gitignore (BlarAI line 114 covers it) - tools/fleet_observability/escalation_seen.json.tmp NOT in devplatform/.gitignore tools/autonomy_budget/state.json IS NOT gitignored (per devplatform/.gitignore comment "state.json itself IS tracked"); the recursive copy from BlarAI carried BlarAI's runtime state (paused, last_updated_utc=2026-04-24T20:46:12Z); committing creates stale snapshot that Stage 4 will replace.
item.severity: Class B (runtime state files committed create stale-state confusion; recoverable)

item.disk_state: STATUS.md Stage Ledger row 51 currently: `| 3 Copy tools | PENDING | | | | devplatform/tools/ populated, smoke test pass |`. Has placeholder columns ready for population. STATUS.md already has Anomalies section + Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section. Append pattern established by Stage 2 (Footnote items 1-12 + ack chain g6-ea6_n7..n9). Existing acks bound this stage: g7-ea1_n11 (escalation_watchdog typo correction), g7-ea1_n12 (selective copy + 4 Stage 6.7.5 tickets). New ack from this audit: g7-ea1_n13 (with 8 sub-clauses).

devplatform/.copy_manifest_v2.yaml does NOT exist. No prior step creates it; Item 3.10.v2 is the only mention. Spec doesn't define schema. Says "LOC counts of every v2-added file". v2-added files per INFRA_DELTA_v2.md and per <v2_updates> blocks at items 3.1.v2 and 3.2.v2: ~36-40 files total (6 from 3.1.v2 + ~30 from 3.2.v2 X-COMPREHENSIVE).
item.severity: Class D (audit/cosmetic only — Stage 5 cleanup will scan disk independently if needed)

item.spec_quote_lines: n/a — new item; F-N11 in 3.2.v2 verdict deferred this
item.disk_state: BlarAI/tools/tests/ exists with: __init__.py, test_project_context.py, test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py, test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py, test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py, test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py, test_vikunja_client_scope.py (7 files total; 6 test modules + 1 __init__.py; __pycache__/ excluded by gitignore). devplatform/tools/tests/: directory does NOT EXIST. Cascade impact: Item 3.4 testpaths block specifies `"tools/tests"` (per recommended dep block); pytest collection would fail on missing directory. Stage 5.1 deletes BlarAI/tools/_vikunja_client.py (chokepoint); BlarAI/tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py imports it; without devplatform copy, BlarAI pytest would fail post-Stage-5 for tests that should have moved with the chokepoint.
item.drift_classification: FILE_NOT_FOUND (CASCADE from Item 3.1 partial-copy) + Class promotion
item.severity: Class C (functional regression; F-N11 promoted from original Class D)

cross_item_cascades.cascade: Item 3.4 (no [project] block in source) → Item 3.5 (verify import requests fails) → Item 3.7 (smoke test transitive imports fail). Resolution: bind n13(a) empirical dep list + n13(b) verify amendment unblocks the chain.

Item 3.7 (no --dry-run flag; daily_digest writes Vikunja task + BlarAI/docs/FLEET_DASHBOARD.md) → BlarAI working tree dirty → violates Stage 3 invariant. Resolution: bind n13(d) substitute --help; eliminates side effects entirely.

F-N11 (devplatform/tools/tests/ does not exist) → Item 3.4 testpaths references nonexistent dir → pytest collection error at Item 3.5 verify → Stage 5.1 chokepoint deletion breaks BlarAI pytest because test_vikunja_client_scope.py still imports. Resolution: bind n13(f) NEW Item 3.4.5 = copy tools/tests/ to devplatform; class promotion D→C.

Item 3.6 (.vscode/mcp.json only) → leaves devplatform/.vscode/ with only mcp.json → F-N9 deferred to 3.6 EA → F-N8 (post-Stage-5.1, BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json invokes deleted bridge) breaks. Resolution: bind n13(c) broaden Item 3.6 scope to include .vscode/tasks.json.

F-N10 (BlarAI/pyproject.toml testpaths line 18 = "tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests") → Stage 5.6 verbatim remove pattern targets only "tools/vikunja_mcp, tools/fleet_*, tools/autonomy_budget" → Stage 5.6 leaves "tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests" → Stage 5.1 deletes BlarAI/tools/gate_stale_cleaner/ → post-Stage-5 BlarAI pytest fails on missing testpath. Resolution: NOT bound here; forward-bound to Stage 5 EA per F-N10 deferral. Reinforced in §forward_carry_to_stage_5_ea below.

guide_response.required_corrections: NONE for EA-1 retroactively. EA-1 STOPPED at Item 3.4 before any Item 3.4-onwards execution; nothing to correct in committed work. All amendments below apply to EA-1's RESUMPTION execution starting at Item 3.4.

new_acknowledgment.title: Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint amendments — Items 3.4 through 3.10.v2 spec-correction binding
new_acknowledgment.preamble:
g7-ea1_n13 binds eight sub-clauses (a-h) covering all spec corrections surfaced by the Stage 3.X comprehensive audit (subagent output at `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_DRIFT_INVENTORY.md`). EA-1 must restate g7-ea1_n13 verbatim with all eight sub-clauses before Item 3.4 resumption.
Sub-clause class summary: A=1, B=4, C=3, D=3 (totals do not sum to 8 because some sub-clauses span multiple classes; e.g., n13(c) is Class A + Class C).
All sub-clauses are REQUIRED. No sub-clause is optional. (Per LA mature-not-minimal motto + feedback memory: hardening followups are non-optional, both documentation AND architectural cleanup. The same standard applies to Class D items in this verdict — "cosmetic" does not mean "skippable".)
new_acknowledgment.subclause:
Spec at Item 3.4 instructs filtering BlarAI/pyproject.toml's `[project.dependencies]` block. That block does not exist; BlarAI/pyproject.toml is pytest-config-only. Spec is non-actionable as written.
AMENDMENT: substitute spec text with empirical dep derivation from the platform-tool import graph. EA-1 SHALL author devplatform/pyproject.toml as follows (verbatim):
```toml [project] name = "devplatform" version = "0.1.0" description = "Claude development platform — Co-Lead / SDO / EA fleet and MCP infrastructure" requires-python = ">=3.11" dependencies = [ "httpx>=0.28,<1.0", "pyyaml>=6.0,<7.0", "mcp>=1.26,<2.0", "jsonschema>=4.0,<5.0", ]
[project.optional-dependencies] test = [ "pytest>=8.0,<10.0", "pytest-asyncio>=1.3,<2.0", ]
[tool.pytest.ini_options] pythonpath = ["."] testpaths = [ "tools/tests", "tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests", "tools/vikunja_mcp/tests", "tools/autonomy_budget/tests", "tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests", "tools/fleet_observability/tests", "tools/fleet_ops/tests", ] addopts = "--strict-markers -v --import-mode=importlib -m 'not slow'" markers = [ "asyncio: mark a test as an asyncio coroutine", "slow: marks tests that hit real socket timeouts (deselected by default, run with pytest -m slow)", ] asyncio_mode = "auto" ```
Notes EA-1 must observe: - `requests` is intentionally OMITTED. Spec mentioned it but no platform tool imports `requests`. Empirical evidence: grep `^(import|from) requests\b` across `tools/` returns zero hits in source files. - Lower bounds pinned to versions BlarAI/.venv currently runs (httpx 0.28.1, pyyaml 6.0.3, mcp 1.26.0, jsonschema 4.26.0); upper bounds capped to next major to prevent silent breakage. - testpaths block is the platform-only subset of BlarAI's testpaths. Excluded: `shared/tests`, `services/policy_agent/tests`, `services/assistant_orchestrator/tests`, `services/semantic_router/tests`, `services/ui_gateway/tests`, `services/ui_shell/tests`, `launcher/tests`, `tests/integration` (BlarAI-runtime only). - `tools/tests` is INCLUDED in testpaths. This requires Item 3.4.5 NEW (n13(f)) to copy the directory before Item 3.5 verify runs. - `requires-python = ">=3.11"` matches Stage 1.7 placeholder; no change. - Order of execution: Item 3.4 (this sub-clause) writes pyproject.toml; Item 3.4.5 (n13(f)) copies tools/tests/; Item 3.5 (n13(b)) installs and verifies. Items 3.4 and 3.4.5 may execute in either order; both must complete before Item 3.5.

Spec at Item 3.5 verify: `python.exe -c "import yaml, requests; print('OK')"`. `requests` is not in the dep list (n13(a)) and is not used by platform tools; verify would fail with ModuleNotFoundError.
AMENDMENT: substitute verify command with: ```powershell cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -c "import yaml, httpx, mcp, jsonschema; print('OK')" ```
Tests the four actual platform-runtime deps. Item 3.5 install command (`pip install -e .`) is unchanged.
Additional defensive check (REQUIRED): after the import verify, EA-1 SHALL also run: ```powershell cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; .\.venv\Scripts\pip install -e ".[test]" cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tools/ 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 5 ```
Expected: pytest collection succeeds for the 6-test module set (test_project_context, 4 test_v_matrix_v*, test_vikunja_client_scope) plus any tests inside the per-tool tests/ subdirs. Confirms testpaths are reachable post-n13(f) tools/tests/ copy.
If pytest collection fails: EA-1 STOPs and reports — this would be a NEW class of drift not anticipated by this audit.

subclause.title: MCP config dual-schema preservation + path rewrites + VIKUNJA_PASS env-var + .vscode/tasks.json broadening

new_acknowledgment.subclause:
Spec at Item 3.6 prescribes copying `.mcp.json` and `.vscode/mcp.json` to devplatform with path rewrites + VIKUNJA_PASS env-var swap. Spec does NOT disambiguate dual schemas (STATUS Anomaly A1: `.mcp.json` uses top-level `mcpServers`, `.vscode/mcp.json` uses top-level `servers`). Spec also does not address F-N9 (devplatform/.vscode/ scope beyond mcp.json).
AMENDMENT: bind explicit per-file rewrites and broaden Item 3.6 scope.
AMENDMENT C(i) — `BlarAI/.mcp.json` → `devplatform/.mcp.json`: - PRESERVE top-level key `mcpServers` (do NOT change schema). - Rewrite `command`: `C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe` → `C:\\Users\\mrbla\\devplatform\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe`. - Rewrite `env.PYTHONPATH`: `C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI` → `C:\\Users\\mrbla\\devplatform`. - Rewrite `env.VIKUNJA_PASS`: plaintext value → `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}`. - All other keys (vikunja URL, args, time server entry) preserved verbatim.
AMENDMENT C(ii) — `BlarAI/.vscode/mcp.json` → `devplatform/.vscode/mcp.json`: - PRESERVE top-level key `servers` (do NOT cross-pollinate to mcpServers; doing so would silently break VS Code MCP loader). - `command` already uses `${workspaceFolder}/.venv/Scripts/python.exe` — already portable; NO rewrite needed. - Rewrite `env.VIKUNJA_PASS`: plaintext value → `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}`. - Each entry's `type: "stdio"` preserved verbatim.
AMENDMENT C(iii) — broaden Item 3.6 scope to `BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json` → `devplatform/.vscode/tasks.json`: - Closes F-N9 (3.2.v2 verdict deferred .vscode/ broader scope to 3.6 EA disposition). - Mirror `BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json` to devplatform unchanged. The Vikunja Bridge Daemon task uses `${command:python.interpreterPath}` and `${workspaceFolder}` which auto-resolve to devplatform's interpreter and root when devplatform workspace is open. No path rewrites required. - Once devplatform has its own tasks.json with the bridge entry, Stage 5 cleanup may delete BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json's bridge entry without breaking devplatform's bridge launch capability. - DEFER `BlarAI/.vscode/settings.json` and other .vscode/ files: not required at Stage 3; devplatform workspace will discover its own venv/interpreter.
`${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` syntax compatibility: confirmed natively supported by Claude Desktop, Claude Code, and VS Code Copilot. LA must set `VIKUNJA_PASS` in the new devplatform workspace environment before Stage 4 (per existing spec's secret_handling clause).
Severity rationale: Class A because plaintext VIKUNJA_PASS in a future-public devplatform repo would leak the credential; Class C because schema cross-pollination would silently break MCP loading.

subclause.title: Smoke test substitution to non-destructive --help invocation

new_acknowledgment.subclause:
Spec at Item 3.7 prescribes: ``` python -m tools.fleet_observability.daily_digest --project-root C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI --project-id $BLARAI_PID --dry-run ```
`--dry-run` flag does NOT exist in daily_digest.py argparse parser (lines 147-163; only `--project-root` and `--project-id` defined). Grep `dry.run` across `tools/fleet_observability/*.py` returns zero hits.
Without `--dry-run`, the command would: 1. Create a real Vikunja task in project 4 (BlarAI Infrastructure) via `client.create_task(...)` at line 178. 2. Modify `BlarAI/docs/FLEET_DASHBOARD.md` via `dashboard_md.refresh(digest_body=body)` at line 193 (writes to ctx.root = BlarAI per --project-root arg).
Both are destructive side effects that violate Stage 3 invariant ("BlarAI source untouched").
AMENDMENT: substitute Item 3.7 smoke test with the `--help` invocation already specified in `` at line 219 of the Stage 3 XML (which the spec was internally inconsistent with):
```powershell cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 python -m tools.fleet_observability.daily_digest --help ```
Expected: argparse usage output prints; module + transitive imports (httpx, mcp, yaml, jsonschema) all resolve; argparse builds. Exit 0.
Functionally equivalent to spec's intent ("verify devplatform copy can run the tool") with NO Vikunja write, NO BlarAI/docs/ modification.
Item 3.7 preflight (BLARAI_PID extraction) becomes unnecessary with `--help` substitution; EA-1 SHALL omit the preflight when running the substituted command. No `--project-root` or `--project-id` flags needed for `--help`.
Forward-direction: any future stage requiring an end-to-end smoke test of platform-tool runtime against Vikunja shall schedule it post-Stage-4 (after MCP repointing) where side effects are intentional.

Spec at Item 3.9: `cd devplatform; git add .; git commit -m "Stage 3: copy platform tools and doctrine from BlarAI"`.
Bare `git add .` will sweep up whatever Items 3.1-3.8 left in working tree. devplatform/.gitignore is a SUBSET of BlarAI/.gitignore; some platform-runtime artifact paths are not covered.
AMENDMENT E(i) — pre-Item-3.9 .gitignore augmentation. Append the following BEFORE running `git add`:
```gitignore
# Platform runtime artifacts (parity with BlarAI/.gitignore) tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/ tools/fleet_observability/escalation_seen.json tools/fleet_observability/escalation_seen.json.tmp ```
AMENDMENT E(ii) — replace bare `git add .` with explicit allow-list staging:
```powershell cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform git add docs/ tools/ pyproject.toml .mcp.json .vscode/ .gitignore .copy_manifest_v2.yaml git status --porcelain ```
EA-1 SHALL inspect `git status --porcelain` output before commit. Specifically check for: - tools/autonomy_budget/state.json — if present and untouched-from-BlarAI-copy, EA-1 SHALL `git reset HEAD tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` then delete the file (it's a stale runtime snapshot; Stage 4 will re-init at devplatform). - Any `*.log` or `escalation_seen.json` files that escaped n13(e)(i) gitignore augmentation — STOP and report.
AMENDMENT E(iii) — commit with Stage-2-precedent body:
```powershell git commit -m "Stage 3: copy platform tools and doctrine from BlarAI" -m "Items 3.1, 3.1.v2, 3.2, 3.2.v2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.4.5, 3.5, 3.6, 3.7, 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.10.v2 single-commit close per Stage 2 precedent. Cumulative-ack inheritance: g7-ea1_n11 (escalation_watchdog filename), g7-ea1_n12 (selective copy + 4 hardening tickets), g7-ea1_n13 (Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint amendments, sub-clauses a-h)." ```

subclause.title: NEW Item 3.4.5 — copy BlarAI/tools/tests/ to devplatform (F-N11 promotion)

new_acknowledgment.subclause:
F-N11 in 3.2.v2 verdict noted that BlarAI/tools/tests/ should have been selectively copied at Item 3.1 per the Stage 3 spec's `` clause but was not. Originally Class D (test-fixture cosmetic). Audit elevates to Class C: pytest collection at Item 3.5 will fail without it, and Stage 5.1 chokepoint deletion creates a forward-direction broken-import in BlarAI's pytest.
AMENDMENT: add NEW Item 3.4.5 between Item 3.4 and Item 3.5. EA-1 SHALL execute:
```powershell Copy-Item -Recurse -Force C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\tests C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\tests ```
Source inventory (verified by Glob 2026-04-27): - `__init__.py` - `test_project_context.py` - `test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py` - `test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py` - `test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py` - `test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py` - `test_vikunja_client_scope.py`
Total: 7 files (1 __init__.py + 6 test modules). __pycache__/ is gitignored and will not enter the index.
Verify post-copy: ```powershell Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\tests -File | Where-Object { $_.Extension -eq '.py' } ``` Expected: 7 .py files (matching BlarAI source).
Diff verify: ```powershell $blarai = Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\tests -File -Filter *.py | Sort-Object Name $devplat = Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\tests -File -Filter *.py | Sort-Object Name Compare-Object $blarai.Name $devplat.Name ``` Expected: empty (no SideIndicator output) — file inventories byte-identical.
Forward-direction note: at Stage 5.1, when BlarAI/tools/_vikunja_client.py is deleted (chokepoint extraction completes), BlarAI/tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py imports will break unless the test file is also deleted from BlarAI at Stage 5. Stage 5 EA shall verify devplatform's copy still runs (tests against devplatform's chokepoint copy) and BlarAI's tools/tests/ removal is complete.

subclause.title: Verify enrichment with -Recurse + inverse-verify of expanded copies

new_acknowledgment.subclause:
Spec at Item 3.8 verify uses `Get-ChildItem ... | Select-String -Pattern ...` which iterates only immediate children (no -Recurse). Per audit: empirical disk state has 0 hits for the BlarAI-only patterns at any depth, so the verify will pass — but the verify is shallow.
AMENDMENT H(i) — replace shallow verify with recursive verify: ```powershell Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs -Recurse -File | Where-Object { $_.Name -match "Task4|P5_|ADR|LEDGER|IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN|TEST_GOVERNANCE" } ``` Expected: empty output. Defense-in-depth against any nested BlarAI-only file that may have escaped a future copy pattern.
AMENDMENT H(ii) — inverse-verify asserting 3.2.v2 + 3.3 expanded copies are all present: ```powershell $expected_paths = @( "docs\governance\fleet-hygiene.md", "docs\governance\merge-policy.md", "docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md", "docs\governance\STYLE.md", "docs\runbooks\F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK.md", "docs\runbooks\LA_OPERATIONS_INDEX.md", "docs\scheduled\wake_templates\sdo.md", "docs\domain8_spike_findings.md", "docs\claude_cowork\01_EA_COWORK_INSTRUCTIONS.md" ) $missing = $expected_paths | Where-Object { -not (Test-Path "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\$_") } if ($missing.Count -gt 0) { throw "Inverse-verify FAILED. Missing: $($missing -join ', ')" } ``` Expected: zero missing entries; throw not executed. If any missing, EA-1 STOPs and reports.
Class D rationale: verify enrichment is defense-in-depth audit polish; current empirical state already passes. But per LA mature-not-minimal motto: cosmetic does not mean skippable.

forward_carry_to_stage_4_ea.preamble: The Stage 4 EA's iter-1 PRIMARY audit (already bound at g7-ea1_n12 as the architectural backstop pre-destructive-cutover) MUST verify the following residual-risk items that this Stage 3 audit cannot catch.
forward_carry_to_stage_4_ea.residual_risk_item: Runtime behavior of platform tools post-MCP-repointing. This Stage 3 audit verified imports but did not execute any tool. Stage 4 EA SHALL run an end-to-end MCP smoke test against real Vikunja from devplatform context post-repointing (e.g., `mcp__vikunja__list_projects` round-trip).

Cross-file consistency of path rewrites. Stage 4 EA preflight SHALL grep `C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI\\` across `devplatform/.mcp.json` and `devplatform/.vscode/mcp.json` and assert zero matches. Any remaining BlarAI path is a Stage 3.6 missed rewrite.

State.json contamination at devplatform. The Stage 3.1 recursive copy of `tools/autonomy_budget/` carried `state.json` with BlarAI's runtime state. n13(e)(ii) instructs EA-1 to delete; if missed, Stage 4 EA SHALL verify devplatform's state.json is either absent or freshly-initialized post-cutover.

__pycache__ pollution. 12 __pycache__/ dirs in `devplatform/tools/` are gitignored but linger on disk. Stage 5 EA SHALL run `Remove-Item -Recurse devplatform/tools/**/__pycache__` before final verification.

Anomaly A3 — `Agents Cadence Monitor` S4U elevation requirement. Stage 4.7 `Unregister-ScheduledTask` + `Register-ScheduledTask` for this task requires elevated PowerShell (LogonType=S4U vs Interactive for the other 12). Stage 4 spec must guard.
Vikunja shutdown gate at Stage 4.0/4.1 (g6-ea6_n8 Option B forward direction). Fleet-paused state must be re-verified, AND Vikunja process must be stopped (V4=1 → V4=0 transition).
Worktrees-empty precondition (INFRA_DELTA_v2 §3.2). `git worktree list` on BlarAI must show only main worktree before any destructive Stage 4 operation.
forward_carry_to_stage_4_ea.escalating_discovery_warning: Per audit subagent §6 residual_risk #6, the escalating-discovery pattern (each iter finds a new class of finding) suggests Stage 4 EA's primary audit may surface 2-3 additional drift classes not anticipated by this Stage 3 checkpoint. Stage 4 EA shall be empowered to STOP-and-disposition new classes as discovered. The cumulative-ack inheritance vehicle remains available; Stage 4 EA may bind new acks under the g7-ea1_n... or g8-ea2_n... namespace as appropriate.

forward_carry_to_stage_5_ea.preamble: The Stage 5 EA's iter-1 SECONDARY audit (already bound at g7-ea1_n12) inherits the following reinforced findings.
forward_carry_to_stage_5_ea.forward_item: 3.2.v2 verdict's F-N5/F-N6/F-N7 path drifts (orphan fleet files, ea_queue, top-level no-ops) remain forward-bound to Stage 5 EA fresh-eyes. No changes from this audit.

F-N8 (BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json post-Stage-5 cleanup) — n13(c)(iii) closes the dependency: devplatform now has its own .vscode/tasks.json with the Vikunja Bridge entry, so Stage 5 may delete BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json's bridge entry without breaking devplatform.

F-N10 (Stage 5.6 testpaths-removal pattern miss for `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests`). This audit reinforces: Stage 5 EA's testpaths-removal pattern at Stage 5.6 MUST include `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests` alongside the originally-listed `tools/vikunja_mcp`, `tools/fleet_*`, `tools/autonomy_budget`. Without this, post-Stage-5 BlarAI pytest collection will fail on missing testpath. Cascade chain documented in §independent_disk_verification §cross_item_cascades.forward_to_stage_5_6.

Stage 5.1 chokepoint deletion (BlarAI/tools/_vikunja_client.py + BlarAI/tools/_project_context.py) requires concomitant deletion of BlarAI/tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py + test_project_context.py (their imports break post-deletion). Stage 5 EA shall verify devplatform's copies still run their respective test suites against devplatform's chokepoint copies, then proceed with BlarAI tools/tests/ removal.

stage_6_7_5_hardening_tickets.preamble: Per LA mature-not-minimal motto + feedback memory (hardening followups are NON-OPTIONAL for both documentation AND architectural cleanup), this audit reinforces the 4 existing Stage 6.7.5 tickets bound at g7-ea1_n12 and adds 1 NEW ticket.

existing_tickets.ticket: Consolidated locked-artifact amendments (Stage 3 XML, INFRA_DELTA_v2.md, etc.) — folds 3.1.v2 typo, 3.2.v2 governance reclassification, AND now 3.0.v2 audit-checkpoint amendments into one posterity XML cleanup.

new_ticket.title: Stage 3.X audit-checkpoint amendments fold into posterity XML cleanup batch
new_ticket.body:
At Stage 6.7.5, fold the following g7-ea1_n13 amendments into the locked Stage 3 XML (or successor v3 spec, whichever is canonical post-procedure): - n13(a): Item 3.4 spec text "filter from BlarAI [project.dependencies]" → "empirical dep derivation from import graph" + concrete dep list. - n13(b): Item 3.5 verify command "import yaml, requests" → "import yaml, httpx, mcp, jsonschema". - n13(c): Item 3.6 spec text expanded with dual-schema preservation clause + .vscode/tasks.json broadening. - n13(d): Item 3.7 spec text "--dry-run" → "--help" (or alternative: add real --dry-run flag to daily_digest.py + refresh spec). - n13(e): Item 3.9 spec text bare "git add ." → explicit allow-list staging. - n13(f): Add NEW Item 3.4.5 "copy tools/tests/" between Items 3.4 and 3.5. - n13(g): Item 3.10.v2 spec text amended with manifest schema definition. - n13(h): Item 3.8 verify enriched with -Recurse + inverse-verify.
LA may triage priority at Stage 6 EA execution time (existing-pattern S-1/S-3/A11 ticket precedent). Ticket EXISTENCE is non-optional per LA mature-not-minimal motto; ticket creation is REQUIRED. Ticket is auto-created via mcp__vikunja__create_task in DevPlatform-Meta project (id=10) at Stage 6.7.5 EA execution per existing 6.7.5 ticket-creation pattern.

required_acknowledgments.preamble: EA-1 SHALL restate the following 13 acks verbatim before Item 3.4 resumption. Restatement format: standard ack-chain inheritance. Cumulative ack count: 12 → 13.
required_acknowledgments.ack: Initial Stage 3 EA-1 comprehension gate iter-1 acknowledgments (n1 through n9).
OQ-1 V0i HEAD drift dispositioned as STATUS-staleness; verdict `3.1-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITERATION_1_APPROVED_STAGE3_EA_1.xml`.
Item 3.1.v2 escalation_watchdog.ps1 spec-correction (option a: hyphen → underscore); verdict `3.1.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITEM_3_1_V2_VERIFIED_SPEC_CORRECTION_AUTHORIZED.xml`.

Item 3.2.v2 selective governance copy (3 fleet-only files copied; 14 BlarAI-runtime files preserved) + 11 noted-for-Stage-EA findings (F-N1..F-N11) + two-layer audit binding (Stage 4 PRIMARY + Stage 5 SECONDARY) + 4 Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets; verdict `3.2.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITEM_3_2_V2_RECLASSIFICATION_REQUIRED_SELECTIVE_COPY_AUTHORIZED.xml`.

Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint amendments — Items 3.4 through 3.10.v2 spec-correction binding, sub-clauses (a) through (h). See §new_acknowledgment above for full text. Audit evidence at `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_DRIFT_INVENTORY.md`.

proceed_authorization.verdict: EA-1 is AUTHORIZED to resume Stage 3 execution at Item 3.4 after acknowledging g7-ea1_n13 (sub-clauses a-h) verbatim.
proceed_authorization.execution_ordering:
EA-1 SHALL execute remaining Stage 3 items in the following order (matches audit subagent §7 RECOMMENDATION):
1. Item 3.4: author devplatform/pyproject.toml per n13(a) empirical dep block. 2. Item 3.4.5 NEW: copy BlarAI/tools/tests/ to devplatform/tools/tests/ per n13(f). 3. Item 3.5: pip install -e . then `pip install -e ".[test]"`; verify `import yaml, httpx, mcp, jsonschema` per n13(b); pytest --collect-only sanity check. 4. Item 3.6: copy + rewrite .mcp.json (preserve `mcpServers` key); copy + rewrite .vscode/mcp.json (preserve `servers` key); copy .vscode/tasks.json unchanged per n13(c). 5. Item 3.7: smoke test substituted with `--help` per n13(d). 6. Item 3.8: verify with -Recurse + inverse-verify per n13(h). 7. Item 3.10.v2: author .copy_manifest_v2.yaml per n13(g) schema. 8. Item 3.9: gitignore augmentation + explicit allow-list `git add` + single commit per n13(e). 9. Item 3.10: STATUS.md append (Stage 3 ledger row + Anomaly entries for any new drifts surfaced during execution + ack-chain footnote g7-ea1_n11..n13 with sub-clauses).
Notes on ordering: - Item 3.10.v2 BEFORE Item 3.9 so the manifest yaml is included in the single Stage-3 commit. - Item 3.4.5 NEW between 3.4 and 3.5 (testpaths must resolve before pytest collection). - Items 3.4 and 3.4.5 may execute in either order; both must complete before Item 3.5.
proceed_authorization.stop_conditions: EA-1 SHALL STOP and report to LA + Guide-#7 if: - Any sub-clause amendment cannot be applied as written (e.g., BlarAI/tools/tests/ inventory differs from the 7-file expected list). - pytest --collect-only fails at Item 3.5 sanity check (suggests F-N11 cascade incompletely resolved). - Item 3.6 path-rewrite results in any remaining `BlarAI` substring in devplatform configs (caught by Stage 4 rr2 preflight; surface earlier if EA-1 detects). - A NEW class of drift surfaces during execution (per audit §6 residual risk warning: 1-2 new classes expected). EA-1 SHALL NOT improvise; STOP and report. - VIKUNJA_PASS env-var substitution syntax is rejected by any of the three MCP consumers (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, VS Code Copilot) — surfaces at Stage 4 cutover but if EA-1 can preflight, do so.
proceed_authorization.success_criteria: Stage 3 close success per audit §7 success criteria (verbatim): - devplatform/.venv has 4 runtime deps (httpx, pyyaml, mcp, jsonschema) + 2 test extras (pytest, pytest-asyncio). - devplatform/.mcp.json and devplatform/.vscode/mcp.json exist with no plaintext VIKUNJA_PASS, no BlarAI hardcoded paths. - devplatform/.vscode/tasks.json mirrors BlarAI's Vikunja Bridge Daemon entry. - devplatform/tools/tests/ has 7 files (1 __init__.py + 6 test modules per n13(f) inventory). - devplatform/.copy_manifest_v2.yaml exists per n13(g) schema with v1+v2 file inventory and LOC totals. - Single Stage 3 commit at devplatform. - Smoke test (`daily_digest --help`) passes. - STATUS.md Stage 3 row marked COMPLETE; ack chain g7-ea1_n11..n13 footnoted. - BlarAI side untouched (Stage 3 invariant). - Fleet still paused; Vikunja still running per g6-ea6_n8 Option B.

next_action_for_la.step_1: Forward this verdict + audit evidence artifact to EA-1. Two files: - `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\platform_separation\temp_for_responses\3.0.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_AUDIT_CHECKPOINT.xml` (this verdict) - `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\platform_separation\temp_for_responses\3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_DRIFT_INVENTORY.md` (audit evidence)
next_action_for_la.step_2:
EA-1 instruction (paste-friendly):
"Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint complete. Guide-#7 has dispositioned your Item 3.4 STOP and bound Items 3.4 through 3.10.v2 spec corrections under one new acknowledgment g7-ea1_n13 (sub-clauses a-h).
Required actions: 1. Read both attached files in full. 2. Restate g7-ea1_n13 verbatim with all eight sub-clauses (a) through (h) as your iter-2 acknowledgment. 3. Cumulatively restate g7-ea1_n10, n11, n12 as standing inheritance per cumulative-ack chain. 4. Resume Stage 3 execution at Item 3.4 per the proceed_authorization §execution_ordering 9-step plan. 5. STOP and report immediately if any of the §stop_conditions trigger.
Fleet remains paused. devplatform HEAD is `3894221`. BlarAI is at the post-Item-3.3 state. You are authorized to write to devplatform/ and to read-only access of BlarAI (per Stage 3 invariant). Do NOT modify any BlarAI file.
All amendments are REQUIRED per LA mature-not-minimal motto. Class D items are not skippable. Restate-only acknowledgment of all eight sub-clauses, then proceed."
next_action_for_la.step_3: Once EA-1 returns Stage 3 close report, forward to Guide-#7 for Stage 3.10 closure verdict. The closure verdict will: - Verify single Stage-3 commit landed at devplatform with expected file inventory. - Verify STATUS.md append captures full ack chain g7-ea1_n11..n13 with sub-clauses. - Mark Stage 3 COMPLETE; transition to Stage 4 (fresh EA spawn). - Activate Stage 4 EA's iter-1 PRIMARY audit binding (already-bound at g7-ea1_n12 + n13 forward-carry).
next_action_for_la.pause_state_note: Fleet remains paused (`fleet_paused=true`, last_updated_by=copilot_agent, last_updated_utc=2026-04-24T20:46:12Z) throughout EA-1 resumption. Do NOT unpause until full procedure close (Stage 6 hardening complete). Per memory feedback: fleet pause is LA-coordinated across sessions; Guide-#7 will not auto-unpause.
